    Custom entries: 0 · Built-in corpus: 0

    OPERATOR REFERENCE

    INPUT ROUTING — priority order
    1. Explicit prefix operator (tel:, zip:, math:, etc.) → forced handler
    2. High-confidence sensitive patterns → SSN / ITIN / credit card / routing number
    2.5. Unambiguous phone format → (NXX) NXX-XXXX, NXX-NXX-XXXX, +country...
    3. Math → equation with = and variable, or pure arithmetic expression
    4. Corpus search → always runs; searches 485+ built-in NJ knowledge packets + your custom entries
    5. Speculative fallback → ZIP, phone, ticker, plate (only if corpus returns 0 results)
    6. City population lookup → final fallback
    PREFIX OPERATORS — forces a specific handler regardless of input
    tel: 973-555-1234 zip: 07102 ticker: AAPL plate: ABC-1234 city: Tokyo bible: MATT 25:40 bible: (blank = random) today: (date + facts + verse) lang: (open phrasebook) tr: hello (translate) math: 256*3/y=512 ssn: 123-45-6789 itin: 900-70-0000 routing: 021000021 cc: 4111111111111111
    KEYWORD & TAG SEARCH Type any word, phrase, or #tag. Common stop-words are stripped automatically. Multi-word tags like #mutual aid or #criminal justice work. Results are scored by relevance — key field matches outrank body text matches.
    Examples: mutual aid · #labor · pine barrens · newark
    PHONE NUMBERS Recognised automatically (no prefix needed) for standard US formats:
    (973) 555-1234 · 973-555-1234 · 973.555.1234
    1-973-555-1234 · +44 20 7946 0958
    Returns area code region for all US/Canada NANP codes + country ID for international. Use tel: prefix to force any string through the phone handler.
    EQUATIONS (linear & rational) Shows numbered algebraic steps. Linear equations solved symbolically. Rational equations (variable in denominator) solved algebraically where possible, numerically (bisection, 60 iterations) otherwise. Includes verification step.
    2x+5=13 · 256*3/y=512 · 10=2y-4
    ARITHMETIC Pure expressions — digits, operators, math functions. No = sign.
    Supports: ^ (power), sqrt(), sin(), cos(), tan(), log(), ln(), abs(), pi, e.
    2^8 · sqrt(144) · sin(pi/2) · log(1000)
    ZIP CODES All 722 NJ ZIP codes with city and county. Exact 5-digit lookup first, 3-digit prefix fallback. Use zip: to force.
    07102 (Newark) · 08401 (Atlantic City) · zip: 07030
    NJ LICENSE PLATES Format validation only — no live DMV lookup. Checks against NJ plate format conventions (1–3 letters + 2–4 alphanumeric, max 7 chars).
    plate: ABC-1234 · plate: JC-12D
    STOCK TICKERS ~130 major tickers with company name and sector. Use ticker: prefix, or type ALL-CAPS after corpus returns nothing.
    ticker: NVDA · ticker: BRK.B · AAPL
    SSN / ITIN VALIDATION Validates format, area/group validity, Bellmawr distribution rules, and pre-2011 issuing state (randomization ended June 2011). No data is transmitted.
    ssn: 123-45-6789 · itin: 900-70-0000
    CREDIT CARD / ROUTING Credit card: Luhn algorithm check + network identification (Visa/MC/Amex/Discover/etc).
    Routing: ABA checksum + Federal Reserve district identification.
    cc: 4111111111111111 · routing: 021000021
    DATA — adding, editing, importing, exporting
    DATA+ button — add a single entry with key, text, URL, category DATA EDITOR tab — view, filter, inline-edit, and delete all custom entries Import JSON — paste or load a JSON array; duplicates auto-skipped Export JSON — download your custom entries as a portable JSON file Export All — full corpus (built-in + custom) as JSON; good for forking COMPILE button — bakes all data into a new self-contained HTML file FEED button — ingest RSS/ATOM/JSON Feed from URL (makes network request; shows privacy warning) TAG INDEX tab — all tags clickable; click any to search it
    JSON format: [{"key":"#tag1 #tag2","text":"content","url":"https://…","category":"labor"}] — category and url are optional.
    KEYBOARD SHORTCUTS Enter send · ↑ recall last query
    Ctrl+K focus/clear input · Esc close overlay
    Ctrl+B bookmarks · Ctrl+N notes · Ctrl+T tag index
    PANIC BUTTON 1 tap → activates NJ Transit camouflage (GHOST theme, innocuous header).
    2 taps within 4 seconds → clears all local data, locks input, and displays the Know Your Rights card.
    KYR button → opens the rights card without wiping data.
    NOTES — WRITING TOOLS The NOTES tab has three rule-based writing improvement tools. Select text in the textarea, then:
    GRAMMAR — flags passive voice, repeated words, filler words, weak intensifiers, run-on sentences, comma splices. Browser spell-check (red underlines) is always active. REWRITE — suggests concrete word-level and sentence-level substitutions. Click any suggestion to apply it. Targets vague phrases ("very good" → "excellent"), verbose constructions ("in order to" → "to"), and overly long sentences. FORMALISE — lifts writing to business/professional register. Expands contractions, replaces casual vocabulary with formal equivalents. Shows a diff before applying. Good for email and business documents.
    🎮 GAMES — open the GAMES tab or type commands in chat · all games have a ⛶ FS fullscreen button
    game: snake — SERPENTINE. Classic arcade. Arrow keys or WASD. game: lexicon — LEXICON. 5-letter word guessing, NJ word list, 6 tries. game: dungeon — UNDERVAULT. Canvas roguelike. Procedural rooms, fog of war, turn-based combat. Arrow keys. Click map first. game: platformer — GRIDRUNNER. Side-scroller with 8 random palettes (6 bright overworld: noon, dawn, sunset, spring, overcast, golden hour + 2 underground). Click canvas first. game: blocks — BLOC BUILDER. Mining platformer with procedural world: overworld with trees, underground caves with ore veins (coal, iron, gold). Mine blocks with Z or left-click, place from hotbar with X or right-click, select slots 1–6 or scroll wheel. Fight surface and cave slimes. Day/night sky cycle with clock. Click canvas first.

    JAC // JERSEY ANARCHO-COLLECTIVE

    v2.0 · FOSS · Single-file · No server · No tracking · Works offline

    WHAT IS THIS

    JAC is a privacy-first, self-hosted field intelligence tool for New Jersey mutual aid, labor, housing, environmental, and community organizing. It runs entirely in your browser — no server, no accounts, no network requests (except the optional FEED import). Everything is stored locally.

    It is part of a broader JAC network: a loose, decentralized structure of people and resources committed to building collective power outside state and corporate control.

    KNOWLEDGE BASE

    485+ built-in packets covering NJ geography, history, labor, mutual aid, tenants/housing, environment, food, culture, politics, healthcare, criminal justice, infrastructure, immigration, activists, disasters, indigenous history, media, religion, military, architecture, utilities, and more. All verifiable, all sourced. Use the TAG INDEX to browse by topic.

    LANGUAGE TOOLS

    The 🌍 LANG tab contains a 200-phrase survival phrasebook in English, Spanish, French, Italian, and German — organized by category (greetings, requests, directions, food, emergency, transport, health, and more). Use the TRANSLATE sub-tab to look up any phrase by keyword. Two built-in games — FLASHCARD (self-paced) and SPEED QUIZ (30-second race) — let you practice any language pair in either direction. The lang: / tr: prefix routes directly to the translator from the chat window.

    ARCADE

    13 playable games: 8 JSON-defined (editable in the DATA EDITOR tab) and 5 factory-coded classics. JSON games have story splash screens, custom win/lose text, and configurable difficulty. Games include platformers, twin-stick dungeon crawlers, shmups, Tetris, Asteroids, Breakout, a Zelda-style adventure (VERDANT KEEP), and Spy Hunter. The ⬇ GEN WAV button in the arcade toolbar generates and downloads a procedurally composed song. Music uses ZzFX/ZzFXM with 7 original themes each 15–30 seconds long before looping.

    PRIVACY MODEL

    Zero data transmission. All queries run locally against the embedded corpus. Notes, bookmarks, and custom entries live in localStorage — your browser, your device, nowhere else. The only outbound request is the FEED importer, which requires explicit user acknowledgment and a privacy warning before firing.

    PANIC (double-tap) clears all localStorage and presents a Know Your Rights card. The single-tap ghost mode switches to an innocuous NJ Transit Operations Console appearance.

    DISTRIBUTION & MIRRORING

    JAC is a single HTML file. No build step, no dependencies, no CDN. Open it in any browser. Share it by USB drive, local network, Signal, or any channel you trust. Use COMPILE to bake your custom data into the file — the result is a complete, standalone JAC instance anyone can use without touching this one.

    For censorship-resistant hosting: serve it as a static file on IPFS, a Tor hidden service, Codeberg Pages, or any static host. The file is self-contained and requires no server-side processing.

    CONTRIBUTING & FORKING

    JAC is released as FOSS. Fork it, mirror it, adapt it for your city or movement. To add knowledge packets: use the DATA EDITOR tab, or edit the INITIAL_DATA array directly in the HTML source. Each packet is a plain JS object:

    { category:"labor", key:"#labor #union #example", text:"Content here.", url:"https://source.org" }

    Categories currently in use: labor · mutual-aid · tenants · environment · politics · history · geography · culture · knowledge · resources · food · nature

    Pull requests, issue reports, and corpus additions welcome. See the repository README for contribution guidelines.

    PROGRESSIVE WEB APP

    JAC registers a Service Worker and Web App Manifest at runtime (no external files needed). On Android Chrome you'll see an "Add to Home Screen" prompt. On iOS Safari: Share → Add to Home Screen. Once installed, JAC works fully offline — the service worker caches the page on first load.

    TECHNICAL STACK

    Vanilla HTML/CSS/JS. No framework, no build tool, no npm. One file, ~350KB, runs in any browser from 2018 onward. The math engine handles linear equations (symbolic), rational equations (algebraic + numeric bisection), and arbitrary arithmetic expressions. The search engine scores by token frequency with key-field weighting and partial matching.

    ▶ MEDIA PLAYER

    Play video or audio from a local file or URL. Playback happens entirely on-device.
    Direct links to .mp4 .webm .mp3 .ogg .wav etc. CORS-permissive sources only.
    ⚠️

    KNOW YOUR RIGHTS — POLICE ENCOUNTER

    IF STOPPED ON THE STREET You have the right to remain silent. Say clearly: "I am invoking my right to remain silent." You do NOT have to answer questions beyond your name if lawfully detained (NJ requires giving your name if police have reasonable suspicion). Ask: "Am I free to go?" — if yes, calmly leave. If no, you are being detained.
    IF ARRESTED Say only: "I want a lawyer. I am invoking my right to remain silent." Do not explain, argue, or try to talk your way out. Do not consent to searches. You have the right to make a phone call. Do not sign anything without a lawyer. Police can legally lie to you. Anything you say WILL be used against you.
    IF POLICE COME TO YOUR HOME You do NOT have to open the door unless they have a judicial warrant (signed by a judge — not an ICE administrative order). Ask them to slide it under the door or hold it to the window. If they force entry, do not physically resist — state clearly: "I do not consent to this search."
    YOUR RIGHT TO RECORD In NJ you have the right to photograph and video police in public performing their duties. Do not interfere. Do not delete recordings — they cannot legally demand this without a warrant. Back up recordings immediately. Send to a trusted contact.
    🔴 LEGAL HOTLINES — MEMORIZE ONE
    NLG New Jersey: 201-656-6767
    NLG National: 212-679-6018
    ACLU-NJ: 973-642-2086
    Legal Services NJ: 1-888-576-5529
    ICE / Immigration defense: 1-844-363-1423
     Write a number on your arm in permanent marker before any action.
    AT A PROTEST / MASS ARREST If kettled or facing mass arrest: go limp passively, do not flee or fight. NLG legal observers (green hats) document — they cannot intervene. If arrested: give only your name. No statements. Request a lawyer immediately. Wear non-identifying clothing. Cover tattoos. Bring ID. Consider leaving your phone at home or use a burner.
    IF YOU WITNESS AN ARREST Document badge numbers, officer names, car numbers, agency, time, and location. Do not interfere physically. Get contact info from witnesses. Report to NLG, ACLU-NJ, or a police accountability org immediately.
    Local data has been cleared. Reload the page to restore JAC. This card is always accessible via the KYR button.
    📲 Install JAC as an app iOS: Share ⬆ → Add to Home Screen
